what_it_does: This file is the package entry point that re-exports named members from many submodules to present a unified public API for the episodic-memory package.
observations: It is a barrel / index file with no runtime code of its own.
It uses explicit '.js' extensions in all module specifiers.
It re-exports a broad set of modules: types, db, embeddings, indexer, parser, search, recall, board, clone, crossings, navigate, summarizer, distill, and paths.
It exports both type-only and implementation modules together.
There are no default exports and no local symbols.
standouts: The use of '.js' specifiers in a TypeScript source file indicates an ESM target.
The export list covers a wide functional surface area for an episodic-memory system.
conventions: Includes explicit file extensions in module specifiers.
concerns: Re-exporting many internal modules without grouping or scoping makes the public API boundary hard to discern.
No indication of which re-exports are stable public API versus implementation details.
notes: This is the public facade of the episodic-memory package: everything a consumer imports should come through here.
questions: Are all fifteen re-exported modules intended to be stable public API?
Why are '.js' extensions used instead of extensionless or '.ts' specifiers?
Is there a reason types is re-exported first, or is the order meaningful?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file's stated purpose is to expose the public API, and it does so by re-exporting the package's submodules.
apparent_themes: Modular decomposition behind a single facade
intention_signals: To give consumers one import location for all episodic-memory capabilities while keeping implementation split into focused submodules.
contribution: Defines the external surface of the package by aggregating submodule exports into a single entry point, making the API discoverable and importable from one path.
summary_for_embedding: This file is the package entry point that re-exports named members from fifteen submodules to present a unified public API for an episodic-memory system.
intention_for_embedding: To expose the episodic-memory package's capabilities through a single import location.
